tools.description: Perform one browser action (click, fill, press, hover, scroll, type, paste, screenshot, tab-close, open, navigate, openMany, capture, captureMany, fillMany, state). For post-action page state, pass snapshot=true or screenshot=true, or call bc_state explicitly.

annotate.description: Annotate screenshot action with ref labels and boxes.

commit.description: Press Tab after fill to move to the next field.

concurrency.description: Maximum concurrent tab navigations when parallel is true. Default: 4.

continueOnFailure.description: Continue on field failure for fillMany.

copyTo.description: Optional auxiliary screenshot copy destination. Primary save remains in Browser Control runtime.

delayMs.description: Delay between keystrokes in ms. Default: 0.

dialog.description: Include pending dialogs for state action. Default: true.

direction.description: Scroll direction: up, down, left, right.

downloads.description: Include recent downloads for state action. Default: false.

fields.description: Fields for fillMany action. Each item requires target and text.

target.description: Element ref or selector to fill.

text.description: Text to type into the field.

key.description: Keyboard key to press. Required for press.

parallel.description: For openMany, start independent tab navigations concurrently. Default: false.

refs.description: Comma-separated refs to annotate for screenshot action when annotate is true.

rootSelector.description: Root CSS selector for scoped snapshot.

screenshot.description: Include screenshot for capture/state actions, or request post-action state with a screenshot for other actions. Default: false.

snapshot.description: Include snapshot for capture/captureMany/state actions, or request post-action state with a snapshot for other actions. Default: true for capture/captureMany, false for state/post-action state.

target.description: Element ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match. Required for click/fill/hover/paste/screenshot.

text.description: Text to enter. Required for fill/type/paste.

urls.description: Array of URLs or {url, label, waitUntil} objects for openMany/captureMany actions.

tools.name: bc_act

tools.description: Explicitly attach to a running browser via CDP endpoint. Requires an explicit target (cdp or port) and never falls back to launch.

targetType.description: Target type hint: chrome, chromium, msedge, electron, or unknown.

tools.name: bc_attach

tools.description: Capture snapshot and/or screenshot evidence from the active or specified tab. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="capture" for lower token usage.

fullPage.description: Capture full-page screenshot when screenshot is true.

tools.name: bc_capture

tools.description: Capture snapshot and/or screenshot evidence from multiple tabs. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="captureMany" for lower token usage.

fullPage.description: Capture full-page screenshots when screenshot is true.

tools.name: bc_capture_many

tools.description: Execute a raw page-scoped CDP command via passthrough under trusted policy. Requires explicit timeoutMs. targetId/frameId are currently rejected; dangerous browser lifecycle, navigation, screenshot, cookie, security, storage, and target lifecycle methods are blocked. For normal page interactions, use click/fill/snapshot/state instead.

frameId.description: Rejected in the current page-scoped implementation. Omit to use the current page.

targetId.description: Rejected in the current page-scoped implementation. Omit to use the current page.

tools.name: bc_cdp

tools.description: Click an element on the page. Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match. Prefer refs from a snapshot for stability. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="click" for lower token usage.

target.description: Element to click: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_click

tools.description: Close the browser automation lifecycle. Managed Chrome is terminated; attached Chrome is detached without killing the user's browser.
tools.name: bc_close

tools.description: Retrieve a debug bundle by ID. Debug bundles contain structured evidence about failed actions including browser state, terminal output, console logs, network events, and recovery guidance.

bundleId.description: The debug bundle ID to retrieve.

tools.name: bc_debug_failure_bundle

tools.description: Get captured browser console entries for a session. Returns log, warn, error, info, and debug entries with timestamps and source locations.
tools.name: bc_debug_get_console

tools.description: Get captured browser network entries for a session. Returns request failures and HTTP errors with URL, method, status, and duration.
tools.name: bc_debug_get_network

tools.description: Run health checks on the Browser Control runtime: CDP connectivity, memory store, proxy pool, CAPTCHA config, OpenRouter config, disk space, browser state, data directories, config validity, daemon broker, and system memory. Returns an overall health status and per-check results.
tools.name: bc_debug_health

tools.description: Detach from the current browser connection without closing attached browsers. For managed browsers, this is a no-op (use close instead). Returns structured detach result.
tools.name: bc_detach

tools.description: Detect or respond to browser dialogs (alerts, prompts, confirms). Call with action=list first to see active dialogs.

action.description: Action to perform: list or respond.

dialogId.description: Dialog ID to respond to (required if action=respond).

text.description: Text to enter into prompt dialogs.

tools.name: bc_dialog

tools.description: List recent downloads for the current session. Returns structured download information including filename, path, size, and status.
tools.name: bc_downloads_list

tools.description: Drop files or data onto a page element. Supports file drop (local files) and data drop (MIME/value pairs like text/plain). Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match.

data.description: MIME/value pairs for clipboard-like data drop (e.g., text/plain=hello).

target.description: Element to drop onto: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_drop

tools.description: Fill a form field with text. Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match. Use commit:true to press Tab after filling. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="fill" for lower token usage.

commit.description: Press Tab after filling to move to the next field.

target.description: Element to fill: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_fill

tools.description: Batch fill multiple form fields sequentially. Fields should be an array of objects with 'target' and 'text'. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="fillMany" for lower token usage.

continueOnFailure.description: Continue filling subsequent fields if one fails.

fields.description: Array of fields to fill. Each must have { target, text }.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout per field in milliseconds. Default: 5000.

tools.name: bc_fill_many

tools.description: Delete a file or directory. Use recursive:true for directories.

force.description: Don't throw if path doesn't exist.

tools.name: bc_fs_delete

tools.description: List the contents of a directory.

path.description: Directory path to list. Default: current working directory.

tools.name: bc_fs_list

tools.description: Move or rename a file or directory.
tools.name: bc_fs_move

tools.description: Read the contents of a file.
tools.name: bc_fs_read

tools.description: Get file or directory metadata (size, type, modified time, permissions).
tools.name: bc_fs_stat

tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates parent directories by default.

createDirs.description: Create parent directories if they don't exist. Default: true.

tools.name: bc_fs_write

tools.description: Write task output under the active session runtime directory. Rejects absolute paths and path traversal.

filename.description: Filename relative to the selected active session output directory.

subdir.description: Session output subdirectory. Defaults to runtime.

tools.name: bc_fs_write_output

tools.description: Generate stable locator candidates for a target element. Returns ordered candidates by confidence (role/name, label, placeholder, text, testid, css).

target.description: Element ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match to generate locators for.

tools.name: bc_generate_locator

tools.description: Execute a registered helper. Requires helper to be active and validated.
tools.name: bc_harness_execute

tools.description: Find registered helpers matching domain, task tag, or failure type.
tools.name: bc_harness_find_helper

tools.description: Generate a self-healing helper. Writes files under data home, validates in sandbox, and optionally activates.

files.description: JSON array of {path, content} file objects

tools.name: bc_harness_generate

tools.description: List registered self-healing helpers.
tools.name: bc_harness_list

tools.description: Rollback a registered helper to a previous version.
tools.name: bc_harness_rollback

tools.description: Validate a registered helper by ID. Returns validation checks and status.
tools.name: bc_harness_validate_helper

tools.description: Highlight a target element visually on the page. Injects a temporary overlay that auto-removes after 5 seconds unless persist is true.

hide.description: Whether to hide the highlight (if true, removes all highlights).

persist.description: Whether to persist the highlight (default: false).

target.description: Element to highlight: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_highlight

tools.description: Hover over an element. Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="hover" for lower token usage.

target.description: Element to hover: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_hover

tools.description: Launch a managed browser instance. Starts a new Chrome process with automation profile. Returns connection metadata including endpoint, port, profile, and provider.

port.description: CDP port number for the launched browser. Default: from config (9222).

profile.description: Launcher profile: 'system' (uses existing profile) or 'isolated' (clean profile). Default: from config.

provider.description: Provider to use for launch (e.g., 'local', 'browserless'). Default: active provider.

tools.name: bc_launch

tools.description: List attachable browsers on the local system. Probes CDP endpoints and returns browser metadata including channel, endpoint, and attachment status.

all.description: List all discovered browsers including those not currently attached.

tools.name: bc_list

tools.description: Navigate the active or specified tab to a URL, replacing that tab. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="navigate" for lower token usage.
tools.name: bc_navigate

tools.description: List recent blocked/aborted network request evidence for a session.
tools.name: bc_network_blocked_requests

tools.description: List privacy network rules, including user rules and built-in tracker rules.
tools.name: bc_network_rules_list

tools.description: Open a URL in the browser. If no browser is connected, attempts to attach to a running browser or launch a managed automation profile. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="open" for lower token usage.
tools.name: bc_open

tools.description: Open multiple URLs as tabs in the current browser session. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="openMany" for lower token usage.

concurrency.description: Maximum concurrent tab navigations when parallel is true. Default: 4.

parallel.description: Start independent tab navigations concurrently. Default: false.

urls.description: Array of URLs or { url, label, waitUntil } objects to open.

tools.name: bc_open_many

tools.description: Evaluate an installed automation package
tools.name: bc_package_eval

tools.description: List package evaluation history

name.description: Optional installed package name to filter evaluation history.

tools.name: bc_package_eval_history

tools.description: Grant a declared automation package permission by kind or index

permissionRef.description: Permission kind or numeric permission index to grant.

tools.name: bc_package_grant

tools.description: Get info about an installed automation package
tools.name: bc_package_info

tools.description: Install an automation package from a local directory

source.description: Local package directory path to install.

tools.name: bc_package_install

tools.description: List installed automation packages
tools.name: bc_package_list

tools.description: Remove an installed automation package
tools.name: bc_package_remove

tools.description: Record a trust review decision for an installed automation package

reviewedBy.description: Reviewer identifier recorded in review history.

tools.name: bc_package_review

tools.description: List trust review history for an installed automation package
tools.name: bc_package_review_history

tools.description: Run a workflow from an installed package. If workflowNameOrId is omitted, the package must declare exactly one workflow.

workflowNameOrId.description: Workflow id, name, or manifest path. Optional only for single-workflow packages.

tools.name: bc_package_run

tools.description: Update an installed automation package from its source or a provided local directory

source.description: Optional local package directory path to update from.

tools.name: bc_package_update

tools.description: Paste/insert text into the focused element, optionally focusing a target first. Supports policy-approved secret:// refs. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="paste" for lower token usage.

target.description: Optional element to focus before pasting: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

text.description: Text to paste or a secret:// ref.

tools.name: bc_paste

tools.description: Press a keyboard key (e.g., 'Enter', 'Tab', 'ArrowDown', 'Escape'). Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="press" for lower token usage.

key.description: Key to press: Enter, Tab, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Escape, Backspace, etc.

tools.name: bc_press

tools.description: List supported browser provider types, setup requirements, capabilities, and risk labels.
tools.name: bc_provider_catalog

tools.description: Run browser provider health diagnostics and scoring.

name.description: Optional provider name to check. Omit to check all providers.

tools.name: bc_provider_health

tools.description: List available browser providers and the active provider.
tools.name: bc_provider_list

tools.description: Set the active browser provider.

name.description: Provider name to activate (local, custom, browserless).

tools.name: bc_provider_use

tools.description: Start a browser screencast recording. Primary screencast artifacts are saved to session runtime; use copyTo only for an explicit extra copy.

annotationPosition.description: Position for action labels: top-left, top, top-right, bottom-left, bottom, bottom-right.

copyTo.description: Optional extra copy path. Primary screencast save remains in session runtime and is returned as runtimePath.

showActions.description: Display visible action labels during recording.

tools.name: bc_screencast_start

tools.description: Get the current screencast recording status.
tools.name: bc_screencast_status

tools.description: Stop the current screencast recording and save the action timeline receipt.
tools.name: bc_screencast_stop

tools.description: Take a screenshot of the page or a specific element. Primary file always saves under Browser Control runtime; use copyTo only when the user requested an extra copy. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="screenshot" for lower token usage.

annotate.description: Annotate screenshot with ref labels and boxes for interactive elements.

copyTo.description: Optional auxiliary copy destination. Primary screenshot still saves under the active session runtime screenshots directory.

fullPage.description: Capture the full page instead of just the viewport.

refs.description: Comma-separated list of specific refs to annotate (if annotate is true). If omitted, annotates all interactive elements.

target.description: Element ref or selector to screenshot. If omitted, screenshots the viewport.

tools.name: bc_screenshot

tools.description: Scroll the page in a direction. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="scroll" for lower token usage.

direction.description: Direction to scroll: up, down, left, right.

tools.name: bc_scroll

tools.description: List all registered local services with their names, ports, protocols, and paths.
tools.name: bc_service_list

tools.description: Resolve a service name to its local URL. Supports explicit bc:// references and bare registered names. Returns an error if the service is unknown or not responding.
tools.name: bc_service_resolve

tools.description: Create a new Browser Control session. Sessions bind together browser state, terminal state, policy profile, and filesystem working directory. Use this before running browser, terminal, or filesystem operations.

policyProfile.description: Policy profile: 'safe', 'balanced', or 'trusted'. Default: from config.

policyProfileEscalationConfirmed.description: Required when creating a session with a less restrictive policy profile than the configured default.

workingDirectory.description: Filesystem working directory for this session. Default: current working directory.

tools.name: bc_session_create

tools.description: List all Browser Control sessions with their IDs, names, policy profiles, and whether they have browser/terminal bindings.
tools.name: bc_session_list

tools.description: Set the active session by name or ID. All subsequent tools that don't specify a sessionId will use this session.

nameOrId.description: Session name or ID to activate.

tools.name: bc_session_select

tools.description: Get the status of the active session or a specific session.
tools.name: bc_session_status

tools.description: Take an accessibility snapshot of the current page. Returns a structured representation with stable element refs (e.g., @e1, @e2) for semantic interaction. Use this before clicking or filling elements.

boxes.description: Include element bounds with viewport metadata in the snapshot.

rootSelector.description: Optional CSS selector to scope the snapshot to a subtree.

tools.name: bc_snapshot

tools.description: Collect compact browser state: connected flag, active URL/title, tab list, dialogs, warnings, per-section status. Returns snapshot only when snapshot=true (off by default). Replaces tab list + dialog + downloads + snapshot calls. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="state" for lower token usage.

downloads.description: Include recent downloads. Default: false (opt-in — high risk under balanced policy).

fullPage.description: Capture full-page screenshot when screenshot is true.

snapshot.description: Include accessibility snapshot. Default: false (compact mode).

tools.name: bc_state

tools.description: Get Browser Control operator status: daemon, broker, browser sessions, terminal sessions, tasks, services, provider, policy profile, data home, and health summary.
tools.name: bc_status

tools.description: Close the current or specified browser tab. Keeps the browser session and automation lifecycle alive. Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="tab-close" for lower token usage.

tabId.description: Optional tab ID or index. If omitted, closes the active tab.

tools.name: bc_tab_close

tools.description: List all browser tabs with their IDs, URLs, and titles.
tools.name: bc_tab_list

tools.description: Switch to a browser tab by tab ID.

tabId.description: Browser tab ID from tab list, state, or open results.

tools.name: bc_tab_switch

tools.description: Execute a deterministic multi-step browser task sequence. Each step runs a browser action (click, fill, press, etc.). Returns per-step results with timing, policy metadata, completed/executed/successful counts, failedStepIndex, finalState.

continueOnFailure.description: Continue to next step if one fails. Default: false.

steps.description: Array of browser task step objects. Each step must match the fields for its action.

items.description: One deterministic browser task step. Use the matching fields for the selected action.

action.description: Step action: click, fill, press, hover, scroll, type, paste, screenshot, tab-close, open, navigate, openMany, capture, captureMany, fillMany, state, or writeOutput.

annotate.description: Annotate screenshot steps with ref labels and boxes.

commit.description: Commit typed text with Enter when supported.

concurrency.description: Maximum concurrent tab navigations when parallel is true. Default: 4.

content.description: File content for writeOutput steps. Empty string is allowed.

continueOnFailure.description: Continue within fillMany after an individual field failure.

copyTo.description: Optional auxiliary screenshot copy destination. Primary save remains in Browser Control runtime.

dialog.description: Include pending dialogs for state steps. Default: true.

downloads.description: Include recent downloads for state steps. Default: false.

fields.description: Fields for fillMany action. Each item requires target and text.

target.description: Element ref or selector to fill.

text.description: Text to type into the field.

filename.description: Browser Control output filename for writeOutput steps.

parallel.description: For openMany, start independent tab navigations concurrently. Default: false.

refs.description: Comma-separated refs to annotate for screenshot steps when annotate is true.

snapshot.description: Include accessibility snapshot for capture/captureMany/state steps. Default: true for capture/captureMany, false for state.

subdir.description: Session output subdirectory for writeOutput steps. Defaults to runtime.

target.description: Element ref or selector for click/fill/hover/type/paste/capture actions.

urls.description: Array of URLs or {url, label, waitUntil} objects for openMany/captureMany actions.

tools.name: bc_task_run

tools.description: Close a terminal session and release its resources.
tools.name: bc_terminal_close

tools.description: Execute a command in a terminal session, or run a one-shot command. For one-shot execution, omit terminalSessionId. For persistent session execution, provide the terminal session ID from bc_terminal_open.

terminalSessionId.description: Terminal session ID from bc_terminal_open. If omitted, runs as one-shot.

tools.name: bc_terminal_exec

tools.description: Send Ctrl+C to interrupt a running command in a terminal session.
tools.name: bc_terminal_interrupt

tools.description: List all active terminal sessions with their IDs, shells, working directories, and statuses.
tools.name: bc_terminal_list

tools.description: Open a new terminal session. Returns the session ID which you must pass to other terminal tools.

cwd.description: Working directory for the terminal session.

name.description: Human-readable name for the terminal session.

shell.description: Shell to use (e.g., 'bash', 'powershell', 'zsh'). Auto-detected if omitted.

tools.name: bc_terminal_open

tools.description: Read recent output from a terminal session.
tools.name: bc_terminal_read

tools.description: Resume a terminal session from persisted daemon state after daemon restart or shutdown recovery.

terminalSessionId.description: Terminal session ID to resume from persisted state.

tools.name: bc_terminal_resume

tools.description: Take a snapshot of terminal state (buffer content, cursor position, prompt status).

terminalSessionId.description: Terminal session ID from bc_terminal_open. If omitted, snapshots all terminal sessions.

tools.name: bc_terminal_snapshot

tools.description: Get resume status and metadata for a terminal session.
tools.name: bc_terminal_status

tools.description: Type text into a terminal session (send stdin).

text.description: Text to type into the terminal.

tools.name: bc_terminal_write

tools.description: Type text into the currently focused element (e.g., after clicking a textbox). Deprecated single-action MCP tool; prefer `bc_act` with action="type" for lower token usage.

delayMs.description: Delay between keystrokes in milliseconds. Default: 0.

tools.name: bc_type

tools.description: List credential vault entries without secret values. Raw secrets are never returned.
tools.name: bc_vault_list

tools.description: Approve a paused workflow node and continue the workflow run.
tools.name: bc_workflow_approve

tools.description: Cancel a workflow run.
tools.name: bc_workflow_cancel

tools.description: Edit a workflow run's state value.

value.description: New state value. Stored as a string unless valueType is set.

valueType.description: How to interpret value. Defaults to string to avoid corrupting numeric-looking IDs.

tools.name: bc_workflow_edit_state

tools.description: Get the event stream for a workflow run.
tools.name: bc_workflow_events

tools.description: Resume a paused or failed workflow run.
tools.name: bc_workflow_resume

tools.description: Start a workflow from a graph definition (JSON). Returns the workflow run state.
tools.name: bc_workflow_run

tools.description: Get the status of a workflow run by run ID.
tools.name: bc_workflow_status
